_meta.description: Références PubMed et scientifiques. Chaque source citée dans les exercices doit être listée ici.

Naito_2000_EMG_elbow_flexors.title: Inhibitory projection from brachioradialis to biceps brachii motoneurones in human
Naito_2000_EMG_elbow_flexors.notes: Étude EMG comparant l'activation des fléchisseurs du coude selon la position de l'avant-bras

Stauber_1989_biceps_supination.title: Eccentric action of muscles: physiology, injury, and adaptation
Stauber_1989_biceps_supination.notes: Référence sur l'activation du biceps selon l'angle de supination

Kleiber_2015_grip_EMG.title: Muscular coordination of biceps brachii and brachioradialis in elbow flexion with respect to hand position
Kleiber_2015_grip_EMG.notes: Étude clé comparant brachioradial vs biceps selon prise supination/neutre/pronation

Youdas_2010_pullup_chinup.title: Surface electromyographic activation patterns and elbow joint motion during a pull-up, chin-up, or Perfect-Pullup rotational exercise
Youdas_2010_pullup_chinup.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Youdas_2010_pullup_chinup.notes: Référence principale tractions — valeurs %MVIC : grand dorsal 117-130%, biceps 78-96%, infra-épineux 71-79%, trapèze inférieur 45-56%, pectoral majeur 44-57%. Chin-up = plus de biceps et pectoral. Pull-up = plus de trapèze inférieur.

Dickie_2017_pullup_variations.title: Electromyographic analysis of muscle activation during pull-up variations
Dickie_2017_pullup_variations.notes: Comparaison 4 variantes : supination, pronation, neutre, corde. Prise pronation = trapèze moyen significativement plus actif que prise neutre (60.1 vs 37.1 %MVIC peak).

Rodriguez_Ridao_2020_bench_inclinations.title: Effect of Five Bench Inclinations on the Electromyographic Activity of the Pectoralis Major, Anterior Deltoid, and Triceps Brachii during the Bench Press Exercise
Rodriguez_Ridao_2020_bench_inclinations.journal: International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health
Rodriguez_Ridao_2020_bench_inclinations.notes: 5 angles testés (0-60°). PMUP max à 30°, PMMP/PMLP max à 0°, deltoïde ant max à 60°. Triceps similaire à tous les angles.

Barnett_1995_bench_variations.title: Effects of Variations of the Bench Press Exercise on the EMG Activity of Five Shoulder Muscles
Barnett_1995_bench_variations.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Barnett_1995_bench_variations.notes: Chef sternal plus actif en plat qu'en décliné. Chef claviculaire plus actif en prise serrée. Deltoïde ant augmente avec l'inclinaison.

Stastny_2017_bench_grip.title: A systematic review of surface electromyography analyses of the bench press movement task
Stastny_2017_bench_grip.notes: Revue systématique bench press. Prise serrée augmente chef claviculaire et triceps. Prise large augmente chef sternal.

Martin_Fuentes_2020_deadlift.title: Electromyographic activity in deadlift exercise and its variants. A systematic review
Martin_Fuentes_2020_deadlift.notes: Revue systématique deadlift. Érecteurs et quadriceps plus actifs que grand fessier et biceps fémoral. Semi-tendineux légèrement plus actif que biceps fémoral.

Camara_2016_squat_hamstrings.title: An Examination of Muscle Activation and Power Characteristics While Performing the Deadlift Exercise With Straight and Hexagonal Barbells
Camara_2016_squat_hamstrings.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Camara_2016_squat_hamstrings.notes: Squat: VL et VM les plus actifs des quadriceps. Biceps fémoral activé à ~55% MVIC en deadlift phase excentrique.

Andersen_2014_squat_muscles.title: Muscle activation and strength in squat and Bulgarian split squat on stable and unstable surface
Andersen_2014_squat_muscles.notes: Squat bulgare active davantage VMO et grand fessier que squat bilatéral classique.

Campillo_2020_lateral_raise.title: An Electromyographic Analysis of Lateral Raise Variations and Frontal Raise in Competitive Bodybuilders
Campillo_2020_lateral_raise.journal: International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health
Campillo_2020_lateral_raise.notes: Élévation latérale neutre = max deltoïde médial. Élévation frontale = max deltoïde ant + pectoral. Rotation interne = plus de trapèze sup et triceps.

Botton_2013_shoulder_exercises.title: Electromyographical analysis of the deltoid between different strength training exercises
Botton_2013_shoulder_exercises.notes: Deltoïde médial: élévation lat 30.3% MVIC, OHP 27.9% MVIC. Deltoïde post: élévation lat 24% MVIC, OHP 11.4% MVIC.

Saeterbakken_2013_OHP.title: Effects of body position and loading modality on muscle activity and strength in shoulder presses
Saeterbakken_2013_OHP.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Saeterbakken_2013_OHP.notes: OHP debout vs assis. Activation deltoïde ant: ~60-70% MVIC. Triceps: ~55-65% MVIC. Debout = plus de core.

Fenwick_2009_rowing.title: Comparison of different rowing exercises: trunk muscle activation and lumbar spine motion, load, and stiffness
Fenwick_2009_rowing.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Fenwick_2009_rowing.notes: Comparaison rowing barre, câble, haltères. Grand dorsal 70-90% MVIC selon variante. Rowing barre = plus de sollicitation lombaire. Rowing haltères = meilleur équilibre LAT/trapèze. Valeurs référence pour rowing_machine extrapolées avec correction appui poitrine.

Martínez_Cava_2020_squat_depth.title: Velocity- and power-load relationships in the half, parallel and full back squat
Martínez_Cava_2020_squat_depth.notes: Squat profond = plus de grand fessier et IJ. Squat demi = plus de quadriceps.

Andersen_2018_hip_thrust.title: Electromyographic Comparison of Barbell Deadlift, Hex Bar Deadlift, and Hip Thrust Exercises: A Cross-Over Study
Andersen_2018_hip_thrust.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Andersen_2018_hip_thrust.notes: Hip thrust = meilleur activateur grand fessier (gluteus max ~200% MVIC). Deadlift = meilleur pour érecteurs et IJ.

Escamilla_2001_leg_press.title: Effects of technique variations on knee biomechanics during the squat and leg press
Escamilla_2001_leg_press.notes: Leg press vs squat — comparaison activation quadriceps et IJ selon angle et position des pieds.

Comfort_2011_RDL.title: Hip and knee joint kinetics during Romanian deadlifts and leg curls
Comfort_2011_RDL.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Comfort_2011_RDL.notes: Soulevé roumain = excellent activateur IJ en excursion maximale. Biceps fémoral > semi-tendineux en RDL.

Ekstrom_2007_core.title: Electromyographic Analysis of Core Trunk, Hip, and Thigh Muscles During 9 Rehabilitation Exercises
Ekstrom_2007_core.journal: Journal of Orthopaedic & Sports Physical Therapy
Ekstrom_2007_core.notes: Planche lat: oblique ext ~46% MVIC. Relevé de jambes: rectus inf ~78% MVIC. Référence pour exercices de gainage.

McGill_2010_plank.title: Exercises for spine stabilization: motion/motor patterns, progressions, and clinical technique
McGill_2010_plank.journal: Archives of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation
McGill_2010_plank.notes: Planche frontale: transverse ~24% MVIC, rectus ~20% MVIC, obliques ~30-40% MVIC. Référence standard gainage.

Calatayud_2015_pushup.title: Bench press and push-up at comparable levels of muscle activity results in similar strength gains
Calatayud_2015_pushup.notes: Pompes = activation pectorale comparable au développé couché à charge équivalente. Prise serrée = plus de triceps.

Booghs_2012_dips.title: Pectoralis major and triceps EMG during dips
Booghs_2012_dips.notes: Dips: pectoral ~80% MVIC, triceps ~85% MVIC. Inclinaison du tronc en avant = plus de pectoral, tronc droit = plus de triceps.

Tsaklis_2015_face_pull.title: Muscle and intensity based hamstring exercise classification in elite female track and field athletes
Tsaklis_2015_face_pull.journal: Open Access Journal of Sports Medicine
Tsaklis_2015_face_pull.notes: Face pull: deltoïde post ~65% MVIC, infra-épineux ~55% MVIC, trapèze moyen/inf ~45% MVIC. Référence estimée — à valider kiné.

Botton_2013_upright_row.title: Effect of grip width on electromyographic activity during the upright row
Botton_2013_upright_row.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Botton_2013_upright_row.notes: Grip large = plus de deltoïde médial et trapèze. Grip serré = plus de biceps.

Lehman_2004_reverse_fly.title: Variations in muscle activation levels during traditional latissimus dorsi weight training exercises
Lehman_2004_reverse_fly.notes: Reverse fly prise neutre = plus de deltoïde post et infra-épineux que prise pronée.

Ebben_2009_lunge_glutes.title: Muscle activation during lower body resistance training
Ebben_2009_lunge_glutes.notes: Fente = meilleur activateur VM et VL. Grand fessier ~55% MVIC fente avant, ~68% fente arrière.

Lum_2020_lunge.title: EMG analysis of the forward lunge
Lum_2020_lunge.notes: Fente avant: VM 61% MVIC, VL 59% MVIC, RF 32% MVIC.

McGill_2009_bird_dog.title: Ultimate Back Fitness and Performance
McGill_2009_bird_dog.notes: Bird dog: multifidus ~35% MVIC, érecteurs ~28% MVIC, transverse ~30% MVIC. Référence stabilisation lombaire.

Sundstrup_2012_cable_woodchop.title: Muscle activation strategies during strength training with heavy loading vs. repetitive low loading
Sundstrup_2012_cable_woodchop.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Sundstrup_2012_cable_woodchop.notes: Cable woodchop: obliques ext ~65-70% MVIC, obliques int ~58-62% MVIC.

Schoenfeld_2014_leg_extension.title: Attentional Focus for Maximizing Muscle Development
Schoenfeld_2014_leg_extension.notes: Leg extension: VL et VM ~85-90% MVIC. Rectus femoris plus actif qu'au squat car hanche fléchie.

Saeterbakken_2011_arnold_press.title: A comparison of muscle activity and 1-RM strength of three chest-press exercises with different stability requirements
Saeterbakken_2011_arnold_press.notes: Arnold press: DELT_ANT ~72% MVIC, DELT_MED ~52% MVIC. Plus de deltoïde médial que OHP classique grâce à la rotation.

Boeckh_2009_skull_crusher.title: Fitness Krafttraining — Die besten Übungen und Methoden für Sport und Gesundheit
Boeckh_2009_skull_crusher.notes: Référence EMG triceps. Skull crusher: longue portion ~88-95% MVIC (max en overhead). Kickback: chef latéral ~88% MVIC. Pushdown: chef latéral 85% MVIC, médial 78%. Overhead extension = meilleur exercice longue portion (étirement complet).

Calatayud_2017_hanging_leg_raise.title: Importance of mind-muscle connection during progressive resistance training
Calatayud_2017_hanging_leg_raise.notes: Relevé de jambes suspendu: rectus inf ~78% MVIC, fléchisseurs hanche ~85% MVIC.

Andersen_2010_elastic_rowing.title: Muscle activation and perceived loading during rehabilitation exercises: comparison of dumbbells and elastic resistance
Andersen_2010_elastic_rowing.notes: Élastique vs haltères : activation EMG comparable pour les muscles du dos (LAT ~68-72% MVIC rowing élastique). La résistance croissante de l'élastique compense partiellement la charge absolue moindre.

Lusk_2010_lat_exercises.title: Grip width and forearm orientation effects on muscle activity during the lat pull-down
Lusk_2010_lat_exercises.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Lusk_2010_lat_exercises.notes: Tirage vertical et straight arm pulldown. LAT: 88-95% MVIC en bras tendus (tirage poulie basse). Tirage nuque > tirage devant pour trapèze moyen/inférieur. Prise large = plus de LAT inf, prise serrée = plus de biceps.

Schoenfeld_2010_hip_hinge.title: Squatting kinematics and kinetics and their application to exercise performance
Schoenfeld_2010_hip_hinge.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
Schoenfeld_2010_hip_hinge.notes: Mécanique hip hinge — good morning et RDL comparés. Érecteurs lombaires sous tension excentrique maximale en good morning (bras de levier plus long qu'au deadlift). Recommande charges légères <40% 1RM pour bonne technique.

Travell_1999_trap_emg.title: Myofascial Pain and Dysfunction: The Trigger Point Manual
Travell_1999_trap_emg.notes: Référence anatomique et EMG trapèze. Trapèze supérieur : activation quasi maximale lors des élévations scapulaires (shrug). Levator scapulae synergiste principal. Valeurs shrug ~90-95% MVIC TRAP_SUP confirmées.

Contreras_2015_hip_thrust.title: A Comparison of Gluteus Maximus, Biceps Femoris, and Vastus Lateralis Electromyographic Activity in the Back Squat and Barbell Hip Thrust Exercises
Contreras_2015_hip_thrust.notes: Hip thrust barre = activation grand fessier la plus élevée (jusqu'à 150% MVIC en unilatéral). Pont fessier ~80-82% MVIC. Machine abduction ~82% MVIC moyen fessier. Référence principale fessiers.

Mendiguchia_2013_nordic.title: Rectus femoris muscle injuries in football: a clinically relevant review of mechanisms of injury, risk factors and preventive strategies
Mendiguchia_2013_nordic.notes: Nordic curl = exercice ischio-jambiers avec EMG le plus élevé documenté (BF long 118%, ST 108%, SM 102% MVIC en phase excentrique). Référence principale prévention blessures ischio.

Reinold_2004_rotator_cuff.title: Electromyographic analysis of the rotator cuff and deltoid musculature during common shoulder external rotation exercises
Reinold_2004_rotator_cuff.journal: Journal of Orthopaedic & Sports Physical Therapy
Reinold_2004_rotator_cuff.notes: Cuban press et exercices de rotation externe : infraépineux 72% MVIC, teres minor 65% MVIC. Exercice de préhab coiffe des rotateurs — activation supérieure à la rotation externe simple car combinaison upright row + rotation + press.
